title: Rivian Q4 2023 Earnings Highlights
text: Rivian exceeded expectations with Q4 revenue of $1.32 billion, beating the Street consensus estimate of $1.26 billion. For the full year 2023, the company delivered 50,122 vehicles. The company reported a loss of $1.36 per share, falling short of the anticipated loss of $1.32 per share. For 2024, Rivian projected production of 57,000 vehicles, flat versus 2023. Adjusted EBITDA loss was expected at $2.7 billion. The company announced a focus on cost efficiency and improving gross profit per vehicle. CEO RJ Scaringe noted that economic and geopolitical uncertainties and pressures, most notably the impact of historically high interest rates shaped 2024 expectations. The company announced a 10% salaried workforce reduction and revealed plans to unveil the R2 model on March 7. Rivian ended the quarter with $9.37 billion in cash and $10.47 billion in total liquidity. Following the announcement, shares declined 17% to $12.75 in after-hours trading.
